text: You came at a good time. What brought you in?

options.label: I was curious about the people here.
I want to see what this place actually is.

text:
That's fine. Take your time and let me know if you have any questions.
Most things in here came from somewhere nearby.

options.label: I’m not sure of what to do with the farm.
I figured I'd see if anyone was around.
I just want to see what’s out there.

text: It’s yours, that’s a good place to start, but you’ll figure it out, how do you think I run this shop?
They are. This is usually a good place to cross paths, I’m the only shop so that helps.
Then go look. The village is small but it holds more than it shows

They've all been here a while. They're worth talking to.
People here don't ask for much. Is there someone you'd want to understand better?

options.label: I think I need to understand myself first.
All of them honestly, that’s why I’m here.
I’d rather see where they live first.

text: Fair enough. These people will still be around no matter what.
Then you'll be busy. Good kind of busy, though.
That makes sense. The houses aren't far, go take a look.
It's not much, from the outside, but it’s soothing” ; “Anything catch your eye on the way in?

options.label: The farm, it felt like it was waiting for me, no one’s taking care of it?
I haven't really looked, so I’m not sure.
All the time. I like not knowing where I'll end up.

text: It's been sitting there. I suppose that answers your question.
Small place doesn't mean empty place, but we’re glad to have one more now.
Oh, you will look, there is not much to do here, for now at least.
